solutions.solution: (c) From figures (i), (ii) and (iv), we conclude that 5, 6, 1 and 2 lie adjacent to 4. Hence, 3 must lie opposite 4 and vice versa.
(d) According to the unfolded dice, cube figures I and II can be formed.
(b) According to the unfolded dice, only figure II and III can be formed when unfolded dice is folded.
(d) According to the question, option (d) is correct because all cube figures can be formed when the unfolded dice is folded.
(b) According to the question, option (b) is correct because cube figures I, III and IV can be formed when unfolded dice is folded.
(d) According to the question, all cube figures can be formed.
(b) According to the question, only the cubes figure II, III and IV can be formed.
(a) According to the question, opposite of 3 is 5.
(b) According to the unfolded dice, from the above unfolded dice will find that face opposite D is A.
